---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# mlfactors

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The goal of mlfactors is to construct portfolios with ML-based and characteristics-based signals

## Installation

You can install the development version of mlfactors from [GitHub](https://github.com/) with:
      
``` r
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("pauloguimaraes871/mlfactors")
```

## Usage

This is a basic example which shows how to calculate element-wise medians of three matrices.

```{r example}
library(mlfactors)
 median_matrix(
      matrix(c(1,2,3,4), nrow=2, ncol=2),
      matrix(c(5,6,7,8), nrow=2, ncol=2),
      matrix(c(1,1,1,1), nrow=2, ncol=2))
```
